_meta.description: Dated US federal tax tables consumed by the tax engine (plan.md §8). Data, not code — update this file for a new tax year rather than touching engine logic.
_meta.verificationStatus: UNVERIFIED — confirm every figure against the IRS annual Rev. Proc. and CURRENT LAW before Phase 4 relies on it. The One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA, signed 2025-07) changed standard-deduction amounts and made the TCJA bracket structure permanent; the standard-deduction figures below reflect that best-effort but MUST be reconciled with (a) IRS releases and (b) the older pre-OBBBA table in ../../Taxes/CLAUDE.md, which currently disagrees (it shows 2025 MFJ $30,000 / 2026 ~$30,600).
_meta.bracketFormat: Each bracket is {upTo, rate}. 'upTo' is the top of taxable income taxed at 'rate'; the final bracket uses upTo: null (no ceiling). Marginal, not effective.
sources: IRS Rev. Proc. (annual inflation adjustments) — ordinary + LTCG brackets, standard deduction
SSA — PIA bend points, National Average Wage Index (AWI), benefit-taxation thresholds
IRS Uniform Lifetime Table (2022 revision) — RMD divisors

socialSecurityPIA._note: PIA = 0.90*min(AIME, b1) + 0.32*(portion between b1 and b2) + 0.15*(portion above b2). Bend points are wage-indexed per year.

2026._note: STUB — populate from the IRS 2026 Rev. Proc. and reconcile with OBBBA before use. Base year for the live projection is intended to be 2026 (plan.md scenario.taxPolicy.taxTablesBaseYear).

fixed._note: Items NOT indexed to inflation — same nominal figures every year until Congress changes them.

socialSecurityBenefitTaxation._note: Provisional-income thresholds fixed since the 1980s (plan.md §6/§8). Up to 50% taxable above the first threshold, up to 85% above the second.

averageWageIndex._note: SSA National Average Wage Index series, used to index a person's past earnings before AIME (plan.md §6). Populate from ssa.gov (AWI table). Indexing freezes at age 60; later years count at face value. Left empty in Phase 0 — filled in Phase 5.

earlyClaimingReduction._note: Actuarial reduction for claiming before Full Retirement Age: ~5/9 of 1% per month for the first 36 months early, ~5/12 of 1% per month beyond that. Encode precisely in Phase 5.

rmd._note: SECURE 2.0: required beginning age is 73 (for those reaching 72 after 2022), rising to 75 in 2033. Uniform Lifetime Table (2022 revision) divisors below — RMD = prior-year-end balance / divisor. Table truncated at 100 in Phase 0; extend to 120+ before Phase 4.
